paper_title: Paleoseismic Methods for Fault Displacement and Event Correlation

methods.description: This method measures the cumulative displacement produced by paleoseismic events recognized in trenches, using both direct displacement measurements and elevation differences of geomorphic surfaces as indicators.

steps.description: Measure the cumulative displacement produced by paleoseismic events recognized in the trench.
steps.searchable_summary: measure cumulative displacement from trench data

steps.description: Compare the results obtained using both direct displacement measurements and elevation differences.
steps.searchable_summary: compare displacement measurements with elevation differences

steps.description: Determine if the paleoseismic history for the time interval is complete based on the fit of the results.

methods.method_name: Correlation Between Events in Many Trenches
methods.description: This method correlates paleoseismic events across multiple trenches to address potential incompleteness due to uneven distribution and preservation of surface rupture traces.

steps.description: Excavate several trenches along a fault segment to reveal paleoseismic events.
steps.searchable_summary: excavate trenches to reveal paleoseismic events

steps.description: Identify overlapping events in different trenches based on age values.
steps.searchable_summary: identify overlapping paleoseismic events by age

steps.description: Use a progressive confining method to constrain the age of events with only upper or lower age limits.
steps.searchable_summary: constrain event ages using progressive confining method

methods.description: This method involves excavating trenches at multiple sites to disclose evidence of paleoseismic events and analyze deposits for dating and displacement estimation.

steps.description: Excavate main and supplementary trenches at selected sites to reveal paleoseismic events.
steps.searchable_summary: excavate trenches to reveal paleoseismic events

steps.description: Divide exposed sediments into units and analyze lithological characteristics.
Estimate coseismic displacement amount by analyzing thickness and composition of scarp-front deposits.
Validate paleoseismic event chronology using TL dating of samples.
steps.searchable_summary: validate event chronology with TL dating
